chatInput.enableLlmFirst: Enable an LLM in the configuration panel first.
chatInput.modelNotTested: This model has not passed a connection test
chatInput.modelNotTestedHint: This model has not passed a connection test. Run "Test connection" in the model configuration.

sessionConfig.lockedHelp: Data source and knowledge selections are locked after the first message in this chat.
sessionConfig.scopeHelp: Controls resources for this chat only. Manage global resources from Workspace Resources on the left.
sessionConfig.agentToolsScopeHelp: Controls MCP servers and Skills for this chat only.
sessionConfig.noConfigurationYet: No configuration yet. Add one from Workspace Resources on the left.

console.title: Task Console

empty.noStepSelectedDescription: Select a step from the center tool card or Trace timeline to inspect duration, action details, and output lineage.
empty.noStepsYetDescription: After you send a question, process steps appear here in order. Parallel tools are grouped under one step.
empty.noOutputsDescription: SQL, datasets, charts, and reports appear here after a question. Open an item to read it and reference the whole or parts.

console.outputsHintExport: Open any output to read the full content and reference the whole or a selected part.
console.outputsHint: Open any output to read the full content.
console.seeOutputsTab: See the Outputs tab for the full list.
console.startPrompt: Send a question to start dataFoundry.

assets.title: Assets
assets.description: Reusable workspace assets for future data tasks and @ mentions.

configPanel.noToolsYet: No tools yet. Test the connection first to refresh the manifest.
configPanel.configuredDataSourcesHelp: Browse configured sources, sync schema, and open table-level previews.
configPanel.startFromGallery: Start from the adapter gallery to create one.
configPanel.uploadDocumentHelp: Upload into the knowledge base for later reindexing and retrieval.

descriptions.kb: Manage documents, indexes and retrieval settings for workspace knowledge.
descriptions.mcp: Manage MCP server connections. Tests refresh the cached tools manifest.
descriptions.skill: Import SKILL.md or .zip packages. Runtime payloads reference skill ids only.
descriptions.llm: Manage LLM model profiles. Runs reference activeLlmProfileId through run_config.

welcome.title: Data Analysis Workbench
welcome.subtitle: Ask in natural language. The agent can inspect schemas, run read-only SQL, and show the full trace in the console.
welcome.footerBeforeAt: Ask directly in the input below, or use
welcome.inspectSchemaDesc: List tables and fields for the active datasource
welcome.inspectSchemaPrompt: Show the tables in the datasource and summarize the main fields for each table
welcome.runSqlDesc: Generate and run read-only SQL from natural language
welcome.runSqlPrompt: Query total orders in the last 30 days, grouped by date
welcome.analyzeTrendsPrompt: Analyze monthly sales trends and identify the months with the largest swings

welcome.title: Start with the workspace map
welcome.body: Left is where you configure resources, middle is where you ask the data question, and right is where you watch the task run and review outputs.

resources.title: Understand workspace resources
resources.body: The left panel covers Data Sources, Knowledge, Agent Tools / MCP, Skills, Models, and Assets / Files. These are the building blocks the agent can use for each task.

datasource.title: Confirm the datasource
datasource.bodyReady: A datasource is ready: {{id}}. This is what the agent will query for the first task.
datasource.bodyMissing: Add or enable a datasource before running the sample task. Open datasource config, create and test the connection, then return to this guide. The demo datasource is the fastest path when it is available.

model.bodyReady: A model profile is available. The model understands the request, helps generate SQL, and summarizes the result.
model.bodyMissing: Quick start needs an enabled LLM profile. Open model configuration, create and test a profile, then return to this guide.

send.title: Send the task
send.bodyReady: The task has started. The agent will inspect the datasource and run a read-only query.
send.bodyWaiting: Review the sample task in the chat input, then click the send arrow. This step unlocks after the run starts.

console.body: The Task Console shows run status, steps, tool calls, traces, and generated outputs while the task is running.

output.bodyFailed: The run failed. Check the model and datasource configuration, then try the sample task again.
output.bodyReady: When the run completes, review the answer and outputs here. You can ask a follow-up question or inspect the trace.
